title: phelypaea lavandulacea subsp. fraasii
intro: Phelypaea lavandulacea subsp. fraasii, also known as Fraas' catchfly, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Phelypaea lavandulacea plant, belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are due to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Phelypaea lavandulacea subsp. fraasii include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making infusions, decoctions, or tinctures from its leaves and roots, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
